channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Artemis II splashdown forecast: Orion's return to Earth looking good, but NASA monitors Pacific storm that could impact landing - ABC7 Los Angeles
item.description:
1. Artemis II splashdown forecast: Orion's return to Earth looking good, but NASA monitors Pacific storm that could impact landing  ABC7 Los Angeles
  2. Artemis II Lunar Flyby  NASA (.gov)
  3. Artemis II crew will endure 3,000°C on re-entry. A hypersonics expert explains how they will survive  The Conversation
  4. NASA releases stunning new ‘Earthset’ image taken during historic lunar flyby  CNN
  5. Artemis II astronauts now under Earth's gravity again after leaving moon's sphere of influence  Fox News

item.description:

item.title: ‘It really bent your mind’: The life-altering phenomenon astronauts experience in space - CNN
item.description:
1. ‘It really bent your mind’: The life-altering phenomenon astronauts experience in space  CNN

  3. Artemis II Headed Home, but Do We Have Neighbors?  Avi Loeb – Medium
  4. Living Space  The New York Times
  5. Shellene Drakes-Tull: From the beauty of space you can’t see the evil stirring on Earth  Toronto Star

item.title: Fireball spotted in Pennsylvania, New Jersey, Delaware traveled at 30,000 mph before disintegrating, NASA says - CBS News
item.description:
1. Fireball spotted in Pennsylvania, New Jersey, Delaware traveled at 30,000 mph before disintegrating, NASA says  CBS News
  2. NASA confirms fireball seen over Philly region skies was a meteor on Tuesday  NBC10 Philadelphia
  3. Another day, another meteor. 5 states report fireball sighting.  USA Today
  4. NASA releases report on fireball spotted in the Northeast, including Pennsylvania, New Jersey and Delaware  6abc Philadelphia
  5. Meteor fireball streaks across sky over New Jersey, Pennsylvania and New York  FOX 29 Philadelphia

item.title: The Moon is already on Google Maps—did Artemis II really tell us anything new? - Ars Technica
item.description:
1. The Moon is already on Google Maps—did Artemis II really tell us anything new?  Ars Technica
  2. 5 Mysteries That the Artemis Missions to the Moon Could Finally Solve  WIRED
  3. Why NASA’s Artemis moon missions are a game changer for astronomy  Scientific American
  4. Even before splashdown, Artemis II is delivering a scientific treasure trove  Science News
  5. Giant Craters and Suspended Life: 5 Marvels and Mysteries on the Moon  vocal.media

item.title: Astronauts suggest naming a moon crater 'Carroll' after their commander's late wife - NPR
item.description:
1. Astronauts suggest naming a moon crater 'Carroll' after their commander's late wife  NPR

  3. Astronauts Dedicate Moon Crater to Carroll Wiseman, Wife of NASA Commander  The New York Times
  4. Moon memorial: Artemis 2 astronauts name lunar 'bright spot' after mission commander's late wife  Space
  5. Artemis II Crew Pays Tribute to its Commander's Late Wife  Vanity Fair

item.title: Remarkable discovery inside museum item after 26 years: 'Stopped us in our tracks' - Yahoo News Australia
item.description:
1. Remarkable discovery inside museum item after 26 years: 'Stopped us in our tracks'  Yahoo News Australia
  2. Earliest known octopus is not an octopus after all  BBC
  3. Scientists Baffled After World Guinness ‘Oldest’ Octopus Turns Out To Not Be An Octopus At All  AOL.com
  4. This Fossil Once Rewrote Everything We Knew About Octopuses. Turns Out, It's Not An Octopus At All  IFLScience
  5. Museum display found to be entirely new species after 26 years: 'Difficult to decipher'  Yahoo News Australia

item.title: How Artemis II’s Earthset photo compares with the iconic Earthrise image from 1968 - The Conversation
item.description:
1. How Artemis II’s Earthset photo compares with the iconic Earthrise image from 1968  The Conversation
  2. 58 Years After ‘Earthrise,’ NASA’s New Moonshot May Rediscover Earth  The New York Times
  3. Fact Check: FAKE Artemis II Photo Shows Earth With Identical Cloud Patterns To Apollo 8 Photo From 1968  Yahoo
  4. Earthrise to Earthset: how the planet’s climate has changed since the photo that inspired the environmental movement  The Conversation
  5. Artemis 2 moon astronauts will try to recreate Apollo 8's historic 'Earthrise' photo during April 6 flyby  Space

item.title: Is the next 'great comet' coming? See Comet PanSTARRS brighten in the night sky this week. - Live Science
item.description:
1. Is the next 'great comet' coming? See Comet PanSTARRS brighten in the night sky this week.  Live Science
  2. Watch Comet MAPS get destroyed by cataclysmic fragmentation near the sun  Space
  3. One of the most important stellar shows of the year won't happen  Notebookcheck
  4. Record Breaking Sungrazing Comet Is On Track To Become The Brightest Comet Of The Year When It Passes By Our Star In April  TwistedSifter
  5. Best Ways to See the "Great Easter Comet"  Hofstra University News

item.title: Christian Artemis II pilot Victor Glover reflects on God's creation from space - Christian Post
item.description:
1. Christian Artemis II pilot Victor Glover reflects on God's creation from space  Christian Post

  4. Sermon on the Integrity: Artemis II mission pilot preaches Christ's command to love on lunar flyby  OSV News
  5. The Heavens Are Still Declaring—and Not Just to Astronauts  The Gospel Coalition

item.title: 'Audible screams of delight' from NASA scientists over micrometeorite impacts on the moon witnessed by Artemis 2 astronauts - Space
item.description: 'Audible screams of delight' from NASA scientists over micrometeorite impacts on the moon witnessed by Artemis 2 astronauts  SpaceView Full Coverage on Google News

item.title: The lengths male octopuses go to protect the arm they need to mate - Phys.org
item.description:

item.title: Japan team discovers why cats leave meals unfinished - The Japan Times
item.description:
1. Japan team discovers why cats leave meals unfinished  The Japan Times
  2. Scientists Think They’ve Finally Discovered Why Your Cat Never Finishes Its Meal  Gizmodo
  3. Why cats stop eating even though they’re still hungry  Earth.com
  4. Does Your Cat Never Finish Its Meals? It’s Probably Bored Of The Food You’re Serving  IFLScience
  5. The reason why cats leave food uneaten has been explained: they will eat it again if the smell of the food changes.  GIGAZINE

item.title: What Is Going On With the Artemis 2 Toilet? - Gizmodo
item.description:

  4. See the Artemis II astronauts give a tour of their ride to the moon  National Geographic
  5. The importance of space toilets, explained  vox.com

item.title: Artemis 2 astronauts spy the Milky Way photo of the day for April 8, 2026 - Space
item.description:
1. Artemis 2 astronauts spy the Milky Way photo of the day for April 8, 2026  Space
  2. NASA Shares "Heavenly View" Of Milky Way Taken By Artemis II Crew  NDTV
  3. Why you can’t see space junk in Artemis II photos  Popular Science
  4. Yes, You Can See Stars And Planets In The Artemis II Photos – Here's Why  IFLScience
  5. Why you can’t see any stars in photos from Artemis II  The Hill
